Blackstone Maul  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 53
DMG: 51 Dmg Bonus: 175
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 WIS: +5 INT: +5
WT: 6.0 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR PAL SHD MNK BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 53
DMG: 51 Dmg Bonus: 175

Page Updated:Fri Oct 2nd, 2020

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55

NPC Name

very nice weapon, well deserved for the groups of fellows that hunt up there

#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 27 2002 at 1:59 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Renovan
RE: Renovan
QuoteReply
# May 12 2002 at 1:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591774066779
Scholar
103 posts
They have pills for people with snap reactions like that... Allow me to sum up what Renovan said (which i happen to agree with).
If a weapon drops on a raid, Not all classes that can USE it might be able to roll, because it would just not benefit them. AKA The Lammy 9/19 6str/sta 20hp WAR RNG SHD usable. No SK in their right mind would use a Lammy, due to the fact that they get much better, aka the ever so common MOSS, 19/29 10int 1hb.
Renovan says the truth, all raid leaders have choices like that to make and not everytime do all the listed classes get a roll.

Wolveran Shadowbane
52 Ranger

Shizadow Knizight
25 Shadowknight

Venetus Salutaris
21 Cleric

#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 19 2001 at 8:58 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) "i dont get why people dont catch on. how many times do i have to say this, THERE ARE NO MONK ITEMS, MONKS ARE HORRIBLE."
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 18 2001 at 4:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i dont get why people dont catch on. how many times do i have to say this, THERE ARE NO MONK ITEMS, MONKS ARE HORRIBLE.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 18 2001 at 6:42 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is sure a awesome monk weapon. *wink*
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 3:48 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Why dont you all just grow up and stop posting stuff about what you think is good or bad. nooone really cares about your specific opinion about this we all want to know stuff like is it fake is it found on a specific creature what lvl you should be and so forth not what you and your guildmates and friends think about it or other classes, BTW NO class is better then the others cause i play them all, and they all have thier place and as for this weapon i have no idea what it dropps off of and i dont care :)
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 17 2001 at 2:19 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) and through that post you completely contradict yourself posting your personal opinion about our personal opinions.....hmmmm
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 12:36 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I'm a monk, and I would let a paladin or shadowknight take this before I did, simply because the stats are more useful for them, with the wisdom and intellligence, plus there's much better weapons that are easier to get (IFS for one) for a monk, so I wouldn't even consider taking this. Just my take on it.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 9:57 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i had better work on my 1hb :P
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 18 2001 at 9:39 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Go right ahead, but I have no idea why you've chosen to post this comment on this item's post, seeing as this item is 2hb. :P
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 15 2001 at 10:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Erm...have any of you that posted been to veeshans peak? Do you have a weapon anywhere near as good as this? K STFU please =D
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 5:20 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) heh, i Have a Tranquill Staff and it makes that look like a Fine Steel Great Staff and I've never been to Veeshan's Peak
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 15 2001 at 10:23 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) that item may dig out some bad damage but still to slow to use.:(
RE: that is to slow

# Jun 15 2001 at 11:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756564350
Scholar
33 posts
HAHA!

Yuz zo funny! At da 43rd lebel, mez, an SK wit only 149 str hits fer da 100's with occassional 115 dmg max mez do believe wit a deathbringer rod dat be 30/40. mez trade arm an leg fer diz maul, if wereznt no droppys. At da lebel yaz git taz git diz, delay don matter, least to mez it don. I don wanna aggro tuz much, datz wat da meat shields er fer. mez put on me grim aura all da time an den buff meself wit 15 pts of da targets attack rate, which bumps me attack up bout 30pts so mez good der. den mez culd debuf da ac az well an hits more but mez lik ta git demz niz an pulpy.

duz i hav point, i dunno. mez jus luv diz an i fer one wuld lub one! not too slow to use. me gitz comments when i use me db rod in a group dat it hits a lot. mez double crak wit lots wit me gud agi or dex, whichever helps :) hahaha dey both bout da same lebel anywayz :)
gimme tell on Fennin if yaz goin. mez go long jus taz git diz tin if yaz don wan it ;P

As good and better monk 2hbs drop in VP..and this has wis too..I'd give it to a pally or SK first, they's get more use from it, and I AM a monk
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 15 2001 at 7:46 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) OK OK I just wanna say Huh?
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 15 2001 at 7:26 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok here is my take on this whole "this is good for a monk/ or what ever class"
RE: OMG
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 6:31 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247994243
Scholar
78 posts
"Ok here is my take on this whole "this is good for a monk/ or what ever class"

If a class is listed as a user, its good for that class. Period, end of story, no discution, no flame. if you can use it, get one, if not STFU"

I'd better hurry out and get a flowing black silk sash on my high elf enchanter. I mean, everyone loves that item, and since it's all/all, it must be GREAT for me too right?

This philosophy applies almost nowhere. The amount of items that are all/all that are actually usable to all/all you can count on literally two hands, and most of those are from dragons or gods. (Cazic's resist ring, the talismans from the dragons in WW, the heiro cloak) The other small handful of items that this philosophy applies to are the resist items. Crown of the Froglok Kings can be used by anyone just because everyone wants to go on a dragon raid one day, and that's definitely the best item for Nag and Vox, though it's still one of the best monk headpieces in the game.

Even in this situation, is the crown worth more to the 47-52 that puts on their crown MAYBE once a week for a dragon raid otherwise it just takes up bank space, or is it worth more to the 1-60 monk who will be wearing that item always.

Puh-lease think before you post.
▲ Page top
RE: OMG
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 4:41 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ok, what the original person said makes sense. They never said that it would be the best item for the classes that can use it, they just said that it would be good for them. Whether or not a person chooses to use it is up to them, and that depends on what they have currently.

and to the person that used that crown example: yeah ok, maybe it's worth more for the monk. that doesn't mean that a Crown of the Froglok Kings should be defaulted to a monk just because of that. it's useful for everyone else as well, whether they use it every day or not!
▲ Page top
RE: OMG
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 11:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752558215
83 posts
It's a decent suggestion, but it just frankly doesn't work all the time. Look at a very common, simple scenerio, if your camping gaurd in Seb and a Lam drops, assuming its' a NbG group, that means (according to you) The Shadowknight has just as much claim to it as the warrior/ranger now think real hard here for a sec (might be hard, i understand) now... even though SHD is listed as a class that can use it... should they get it? NO, SK's have much better weapons, and would be fools to use this over something as basic as a MoSS. So I'm affraid that philosophy does not work..on all the good raids i have been a part of, the raid leader looks at the item and does not (all the time) let ever class who can use it, roll on it. For very simple reason, certain classes benefit a lot more. Now true, it is stupid how much arguing and ******** goes on on this board, I can tell all those people have never themselves been on a raid where situations involving loot have arrised (that or they are loot whores, a very common thing) but your suggestion of "If a class is listed as a user, its good for that class." is completley erronious. Thank you.

-Renovan, 55 Warrior of Xev
▲ Page top
RE: OMG
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2001 at 4:02 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I act as raid officer for my guild, and my primary function is to handle item lottos. I am tired of hearing what's best for what class. It's what works best for the player. If there was an ultimate combination for each class (armor, weapon, items, jewelrey) then this game would be no fun. In short- those of you who claim to be all and know all- shut the f### up. Go home and try to figure out how to type "I suck" only using symbols......idiots.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 12:12 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) "If a class is listed as a user, its good for that class. Period."
RE: OMG

# Jun 16 2001 at 6:01 PM Rating: Default

Anonymous
If the player has nothing on him in his secoundary slot will it increase his stats? Yes. It's not as good as other items, but it can help any class.
